data.value: World number one and clear favorite at 7/2. Most consistent player on tour right now. Has a difficult head-to-head record against defending champion Kyren Wilson this season.

Four-time World Champion with almost unparalleled combination of tactical acumen, break-building consistency, and mental resilience. Demonstrated excellent late-season form. The Crucible suits his grinding style.

Defending champion faces the famous Crucible Curse — no first-time winner has successfully defended the title since the tournament moved to Sheffield in 1977. However, Wilson's current form and head-to-head record against Trump are strong.
Won the 2026 Masters creating a near-impossible snooker record per analysts. Current form is arguably among the best of his career, making him dangerous at 7/1.
Seven-time world champion listed at 20/1 reflecting concerns over age and inconsistent recent form. Still dangerous at the Crucible but bookmakers view a ninth title as unlikely.
